# Prepare normalization functions and search
import re, unicodedata

arabic_diacritics = re.compile("""
                             ّ    | # Shadda
                             َ    | # Fatha
                             ً    | # Tanwin Fath
                             ُ    | # Damma
                             ٌ    | # Tanwin Damm
                             ِ    | # Kasra
                             ٍ    | # Tanwin Kasr
                             ْ    | # Sukun
                             ـ     # Tatwil/Kashida
                         """, re.VERBOSE)

punctuation = re.compile(r"[\s\-\—\–\:\,\.\;\؟\؟\!\(\)\"'«»\<\>\/\[\]\{\}\…\ـ]")

def normalize_ar(text):
    if text is None:
        return ""
    # ensure string
    s = str(text)
    # remove diacritics
    s = arabic_diacritics.sub("", s)
    # normalize hamza and alef variants
    s = s.replace('آ','ا').replace('أ','ا').replace('إ','ا')
    s = s.replace('ؤ','و').replace('ئ','ي')
    # remove tatweel already in diacritics removal maybe
    s = s.replace('ـ','')
    # remove punctuation and spaces collapsed to single space
    # But for matching we might want to remove all punctuation and collapse spaces
    s = re.sub(r'[^ء-ي0-9\s]', '', s)  # keep Arabic letters and numbers and spaces (this removes punctuation)
    # convert Arabic-Indic digits to ASCII? not necessary
    s = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', s).strip()
    # lower not necessary for Arabic
    return s

# Tokens
def tokens(text):
    s = normalize_ar(text)
    return s.split()

# Similarity metrics
def token_overlap_score(a, b):
    # F1 score between tokens
    ta = tokens(a)
    tb = tokens(b)
    if not ta or not tb:
        return 0.0
    set_a = ta
    set_b = tb
    # count common tokens (with multiplicity)
    from collections import Counter
    ca = Counter(set_a)
    cb = Counter(set_b)
    common = sum(min(ca[t], cb[t]) for t in ca if t in cb)
    precision = common / len(tb)
    recall = common / len(ta)
    if precision+recall == 0:
        return 0.0
    f1 = 2*precision*recall/(precision+recall)
    return f1

# Longest common substring length normalized
def lcs_length(a,b):
    # compute longest common substring length on normalized strings (without spaces)
    na = normalize_ar(a).replace(" ","")
    nb = normalize_ar(b).replace(" ","")
    if not na or not nb:
        return 0
    # dynamic programming
    m = len(na)
    n = len(nb)
    dp = [0]*(n+1)
    longest = 0
    for i in range(1, m+1):
        newdp = [0]*(n+1)
        for j in range(1, n+1):
            if na[i-1] == nb[j-1]:
                newdp[j] = dp[j-1] + 1
                if newdp[j] > longest:
                    longest = newdp[j]
        dp = newdp
    return longest

def lcs_ratio(a,b):
    na = normalize_ar(a).replace(" ","")
    nb = normalize_ar(b).replace(" ","")
    if not na or not nb:
        return 0.0
    l = lcs_length(a,b)
    # ratio vs longer of the two
    denom = max(len(na), len(nb))
    return l/denom

# test normalization

print(tokens(test))
print(token_overlap_score(test, "أثناء الهجرة قال أبو بكر ائذن لي أن أستقدم أمامك"))
print(lcs_ratio(test, "أثناء الهجرة قال أبو بكر ائذن لي أن أستقدم أمامك"))

candidates = []
for entry in data['hadith']:
    # get text field: prefer hadithTxt then Matn
    text = entry.get('hadithTxt') or entry.get('Matn') or ""
    if not text:
        continue
    # compute similarities
    to_score = token_overlap_score(query_full, text)
    lcs_r = lcs_ratio(query_full, text)
    # also check if normalized query substring is substring of text
    norm_q = normalize_ar(query_full)
    norm_t = normalize_ar(text)
    is_sub = norm_q in norm_t or any(tok in norm_t for tok in normalize_ar("".join(query_full)).split()) # not necessary
    # record
    candidates.append((to_score, lcs_r, is_sub, entry, text[:200]))
# sort by token overlap descending
